purpose: Test whether the bounded incomparable-kernel result survives genuinely external criticism, independent implementation, new candidate architectures, new contexts, and empirical correspondence requirements.

workstreams.required_independence: eligible reviewer uninvolved in package construction
separate human or organization and independently implemented pipeline
adversarial conceptual team permitted to challenge scope, equivalence, costs, and candidate construction

workstreams.minimum_requirement: at least one materially new non-FARA and non-LTS-PROV architecture selected independently of current winners
new reasoning contexts not used to construct the frozen benchmark
independent observations, measurement error model, state bridge, alternative-process controls, and discriminating interventions

decision_rules: internal implementation diversity does not satisfy external independence
review packages may not be repaired after reviewer access without a new version
candidate expansion may change the minimal frontier
failure to find a new candidate is not proof of exhaustiveness
cross-context failure limits scope rather than being discarded
formal fit alone cannot establish actual-process correspondence
nonclaims: the current frontier is stable under candidate expansion
